content:
# Ephesians Chapter 3 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Date:** 2026-03-05

---

### v.2 — "the administration of the grace" vs. "the stewardship of God's grace" vs. "the special responsibility of extending his grace"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the administration of the grace of God having been given to me" | oikonomian tēs charitos — "administration/stewardship" |
| LOGOS | "the stewardship of God's grace that was given to me" | "Stewardship" — same |
| PNEUMA | "God gave me the special responsibility of extending his grace to you Gentiles" | "Special responsibility of extending" — paraphrase |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. oikonomia means "household management/stewardship/administration." PNEUMA expands into "special responsibility of extending" — adding "extending" (not in Greek).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "extending."

---

### v.5 — "the sons of men" vs. "the sons of men" vs. absence

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "was not made known to the sons of men in other generations" | tois huiois tōn anthrōpōn — Hebraism |
| LOGOS | "was not made known to the sons of men in other generations" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "God did not reveal it to previous generations" | Drops "sons of men"; restructures |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. "Sons of men" (huioi tōn anthrōpōn) is a Hebraism meaning "human beings." PNEUMA drops the phrase entirely, losing both the idiom and its echo of OT usage (especially Ezekiel).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA drops Hebraism.

---

### v.6 — Three syn- compounds rendering

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "fellow heirs and fellow members of [the] body and fellow partakers of the promise" | Preserves three syn- prefixes |
| LOGOS | "fellow heirs, members of the same body, and partakers of the promise" | "Same body" renders syssōma |
| PNEUMA | "Both Gentiles and Jews who believe the Good News share equally in the riches inherited by God's children. Both are part of the same body, and both enjoy the promise of blessings" | Full expansion; adds "who believe the Good News," "equally," "riches," "of blessings" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA massively expands, adding "who believe the Good News," "equally," "the riches inherited by God's children," and "of blessings." Paul's verse is compressed: three technical terms in one sentence. PNEUMA turns it into three separate sentences with significant additions.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA massively expands compressed formula.

---

### v.8 — "the least of all holy ones" vs. "the very least of all the saints" vs. "the least deserving of all God's people"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the least of all holy [ones]" | elachistoterō — double comparative |
| LOGOS | "the very least of all the saints" | "Very least" — captures the double comparative |
| PNEUMA | "the least deserving of all God's people" | Adds "deserving"; "God's people" for hagioi |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. elachistoteros is a grammatically impossible double comparative — "leaster than the least." LOGOS's "very least" captures the intensification. PNEUMA adds "deserving" (not in Greek — Paul says he is the least, not the least deserving).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "deserving."

---

### v.9 — "the administration of the mystery" vs. "the plan of the mystery" vs. "this mysterious plan"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "what [is] the administration of the mystery having been hidden" | oikonomia tou mystēriou — "stewardship/plan of the mystery" |
| LOGOS | "what is the plan of the mystery hidden for ages in God" | "Plan" for oikonomia |
| PNEUMA | "this mysterious plan that God, the Creator of all things, had kept secret from the beginning" | "Mysterious plan" — combines; "from the beginning" for "from the ages" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA combines oikonomia and mystērion into "mysterious plan" — losing the distinction between the "plan/administration" and the "mystery." PNEUMA also changes "from the ages" (apo tōn aiōnōn) to "from the beginning."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA conflates two distinct terms.

---

### v.10 — "the manifold wisdom of God" vs. "his wisdom in its rich variety" and audience

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known through the assembly to the rulers and the authorities in the heavenly [places]" | polypoikilos — "many-colored/manifold" |
| LOGOS | "the manifold wisdom of God might now be made known to the rulers and authorities in the heavenly places" | Same |
| PNEUMA | "God's purpose in all this was to use the church to display his wisdom in its rich variety to all the unseen rulers and authorities in the heavenly places" | "Display"; "rich variety" for polypoikilos; adds "unseen" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. polypoikilos means "much-variegated/manifold" — used in classical Greek for embroidered cloth with many colors. LOGOS preserves "manifold." PNEUMA's "rich variety" is adequate but adds "unseen" (not in Greek) to "rulers and authorities."

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA adds "unseen."

---

### v.12 — "the faith of him" vs. "our faith in him" vs. "our faith in him"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "through the faith of him" | dia tēs pisteōs autou — genitive |
| LOGOS | "through our faith in him" | Objective genitive |
| PNEUMA | "Because of Christ and our faith in him" | Adds "Because of Christ and" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Same pistis + genitive issue as Galatians. LOGOS renders as objective genitive ("faith in him"). PNEUMA adds "Because of Christ and" — expanding a single prepositional phrase into a dual cause.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. pistis autou — same genitive debate.

---

### v.15 — "from whom every family...is named" vs. "the Creator and Father of everything"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "from whom every family in [the] heavens and upon [the] earth is named" | patria...onomazetai — wordplay with patēr |
| LOGOS | "from whom every family in heaven and on earth is named" | Same — preserves wordplay |
| PNEUMA | "the Creator and Father of everything in heaven and on earth" | Loses wordplay; "Creator and Father of everything" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. The Greek contains a deliberate wordplay: patēr (Father) → patria (family/clan). Every family derives its identity from the Father. PNEUMA replaces this with "Creator and Father of everything" — losing the patēr/patria wordplay entirely.

**Status:** ⚠️ Theologically significant. The patēr/patria wordplay — all family/kinship derives from the Fatherhood of God. PNEUMA's rendering loses a central theological point about the origin of kinship.

---

### v.16 — "in the inner man" vs. "in your inner being" vs. "with inner strength"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "to be strengthened with power through his Spirit in the inner man" | eis ton esō anthrōpon — "the inner person" |
| LOGOS | "to be strengthened with power through his Spirit in your inner being" | "Inner being" — avoids gendered "man" |
| PNEUMA | "he will empower you with inner strength through his Spirit" | "Inner strength" — adjective, not location |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul uses "the inner person" (ho esō anthrōpos) — a location where strengthening occurs. PNEUMA converts the location to a quality ("inner strength"), losing the Pauline concept of the "inner person" as a site of divine action (cf. Rom 7:22, 2 Cor 4:16).

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA loses "inner person" concept.

---

### v.18-19 — "the breadth and length and height and depth" — object unspecified vs. specified

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "what [is] the breadth and length and height and depth, and to know the love of the Christos" | Object unspecified; love of Christ in separate clause |
| LOGOS | "what is the breadth and length and height and depth, and to know the love of Christ" | Same — preserves open object |
| PNEUMA | "how wide, how long, how high, and how deep his love is. May you experience the love of Christ" | Identifies object as "his love" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. Paul deliberately does not specify what has these four dimensions. PNEUMA identifies it as "his love" — the most common interpretation but one that Paul intentionally leaves open. The mystery may have cosmic dimensions that exceed any single referent.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. PNEUMA specifies what Paul leaves unspecified.

---

### v.20 — "far beyond all things above what we ask or think" vs. "far more abundantly" vs. "infinitely more"

| Translator | Rendering | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "to do far beyond all [things] above what we ask or think" | hyper ek perissou — "surpassingly beyond excess" |
| LOGOS | "to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think" | Standard rendering |
| PNEUMA | "to accomplish infinitely more than we might ask or think" | "Infinitely" — absolute; "accomplish" |

**SOPHIA's resolution:** Adopted LOGOS. hyper ek perissou is a triple-stacked intensifier (hyper + ek + perissou). "Far more abundantly" captures the excess. PNEUMA's "infinitely" is hyperbolically correct in spirit but introduces a mathematical concept not in the Greek.

**Status:** Resolved in favor of LOGOS. Minor — degree of intensification.

---

## Summary of Disagreement Patterns

| Pattern | Count | Description |
|---|---|---|
| PNEUMA interpretive additions | 5 | "Extending," "deserving," "unseen," "Because of Christ and," "his love" |
| PNEUMA structural changes | 3 | Expands compressed formulas (v.6), conflates terms (v.9), specifies open referent (v.18) |
| patēr/patria wordplay | 1 | PNEUMA loses Father/family wordplay (v.15) |
| "Inner person" concept | 1 | Location vs. quality — "inner being" vs. "inner strength" (v.16) |
| pistis genitive | 1 | "Faith of him" vs. "faith in him" (v.12) |

**Overall pattern:** Chapter 3's most significant disagreement is PNEUMA's loss of the patēr/patria wordplay (v.15), which eliminates a central theological point about the divine origin of all kinship. PNEUMA also continues the pattern of specifying what Paul leaves deliberately open (the four dimensions in v.18) and expanding compressed theological formulas into explanatory paraphrases (v.6).

---

*— SOPHIA, AI Translator, in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA*
